CircumSpectrum is a flux-based framework that converts bulk and single-cell RNA-seq data into metabolic fingerprints using genome-scale models and machine learning. It includes RADAR, SOURIS, and CORALIE modules for reaction-level, cross-cohort, and multi-tier correlation analyses.

This project implements a hand gesture recognition system using computer vision and machine learning techniques. It detects and classifies hand gestures from images or real-time webcam input, enabling touchless humancomputer interaction. The system is built using Python with libraries such as OpenCV and deep learning frameworks.

A joint modeling framework of Multi-Task Learning (MTL) and machine learning for limited sample data is proposed. This method integrates a hybrid neural network model that combines a Multilayer Perceptron and Convolutional Neural Network. It incorporates a cross-stitch structure, MTL, and homoscedastic uncertainty loss weighting mechanism.

A distributed machine learning framework designed for real-world edge deployments where devices are resource-constrained, connections are unreliable and trust cannot be assumed. By using Replay Memory and Exemplar Datasets, the system evolves to recognize new machine types over time without losing its ability to monitor previous ones.

Extra Trees achieved best performance with RMSE  4.1 and R  0.95 for accurate textile manufacturing prediction.

A modular Python framework for fairness-aware post-processing in machine learning.  The library takes classifier probabilities and learns optimal decision thresholds  by optimizing multiple fairness and performance metrics, enabling multi-objective  selection via methods such as TOPSIS and Zenith.

SparseBank is a machine-learningassisted framework for reducing matched-filter template banks. By learning the object masses it allows to significantly reduce the CBC templates that preserve detection sensitivity while significantly lowering computational cost.

MILP_GNN is a Python research codebase for learning branching strategies in Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) using machine learning. The project implements graph convolutional neural networks and multilayer perceptrons to predict effective branching variables within a branch-and-bound solver framework.

This project develops a multimodal machine learning framework to forecast post-meal glucose spikes across multiple horizons of 1, 3, and 6 hours. By utilizing the CGMacros dataset, the system integrates continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data, nutritional features, clinical biomarkers, and AI-derived food image embeddings.

Predictive maintenance system for rotating machinery using ESP32-based vibration and temperature monitoring.
